metadata.description: Events exported from database - always most updated version

11.title: Grandma Moses: A Good Day’s Work
11.description: Grandma Moses: A Good Day’s Worksheds new light on a beloved body of work by Anna Mary Robertson​“Grandma” Moses(1860–1961). Grandma Moses used creativity, hope, and togetherness as tools for shaping a life that she metaphorically likened to​“a good day’s work.” The artist’s fame made her a polarizing figure — beloved by the popular press and American public but belittled by the art world and critical press. This exhibition introduces the artist to new generations and examines her legacy in the context of America today.

12.title: Shahzia Sikander: The Last Post
12.description: Through precisely inked and animated scenes,Shahzia Sikander’svideo artworkThe Last Post(2010) critically considers the legacy of British colonialism in Asia, using her signature approach of infusing Indo-Persian miniature paintings with a contemporary perspective. Located at administered by the Smithsonian Asian Pacific American Center. This exhibition received funding from the HomeSpun Initiative Fund, administered by the Smithsonian Asian Pacific American Center. This exhibition received funding from the HomeSpun Initiative Fund.

13.title: Glenn Kaino: Bridge
13.description: One of the most memorable images from the1968Olympic Games in Mexico City is the symbolic act of protest by Tommie Smith, winner of the men’s200-meter race. During the medal ceremony, Smith bowed his head and raised his fist as an assertion of Black solidarity in the fight for human rights. Decades later, artist Glenn Kaino collaborated with Smith to create the monumental sculptureBridge. Located at Luce Foundation Center, 3rd Floor Ongoing Open Daily.

14.title: Isaac Julien: Lessons of the Hour — Frederick Douglass
14.description: Sir Isaac Julien’s moving image installationLessons of the Hour(2019) interweaves period reenactments across five screens to create a vivid picture of nineteenth-century activist, writer, orator, and philosopher Frederick Douglass. Through critical research, fictional reconstruction, and a marriage of poetic image and sound, Julien asserts Douglass’ enduring lessons of justice, abolition, and freedom that remain just as relevant today.

15.title: Galleries for Modern and Contemporary Art
15.description: The Smithsonian American Art Museum’s reopened and reimagined modern and contemporary galleries feature a new installation that freshly examines the explosion of possibility in American art between the1940s and today. Nearly100works by artists using new materials and techniques — and inspired by the social, cultural, and technological changes around them — are on view. (Ongoing exhibition)

16.title: Artist to Artist
16.description: The work of the artist is often thought of as solitary. We picture the painter confronting a blank canvas alone, studio door figuratively shut. Yet few artists thrive in a social vacuum. Even those who prefer to work in private will seek out other artists for myriad reasons: mentorship and inspiration, practical assistance, a sense of solidarity or shared purpose. Artists are often each other’s first and most important audience, providing vital support before critics, curators, and collectors arrive on a scene. Two artists caring about one another’s work is fundamental to the creation of any art​“world,” large or small.

17.title: Galleries for Folk and Self-Taught Art
17.description: SAAM’s collection of folk and self-taught art represents the powerful vision of America’s untrained and vernacular artists. Represented in the museum’s collection are pieces that draw on tradition — such as quilts — as well as artworks that reveal a more personal vision. The museum has reimagined its permanent collection galleries for art by untrained artists, which now display several dozen recent acquisitions and an expanded presentation of the belovedThrone of the Third Heaven of the Nations Millennium General AssemblybyJames Hampton. Located at st floor West Ongoing Open Daily, 11:30 a.m.–7:00 p.m Free Admission.

18.title: Experience America
18.description: The1930s was a heady time for artists in America. Through President Franklin Roosevelt’s New Deal programs, the federal government paid them to paint and sculpt and urged them to look to the nation’s land and people for subjects. For the next decade — until World War II brought support to a halt — the country’s artists captured the beauty of the landscape, the industry of America’s working people, and a sense of community shared in towns large and small despite the Great Depression. Located at st floor South Ongoing Open Daily, 11:30 a.m.–7:00 p.m Free Admission.

112.title: State Fairs: Growing American Craft
112.description: Step right up to the first major exhibition to survey American state fairs’ extraordinary and unconventional crafts from the nineteenth century to the present. The exhibition includes exceptional examples of American craft, highlighting personal stories and regional and cultural traditions. Located at Renwick Gallery August 22, 2025 – September 7.

113.title: Justin Favela: Capilla de Maíz (Maize Chapel)
113.description: Capilla de Maíz (Maize Chapel), a site-specific installation by Justin Favela, transforms the Rubenstein Grand Salon at SAAM’s Renwick Gallery into a fantastical world, with shimmering gold-fringed walls and piñata corncobs that highlight the role of maize in North American visual culture. Located at Renwick Gallery Rubenstein Grand Salon Ongoing Open Daily, 10:00 a.m.–5:30 p.m Free Admission.

494.title: Nü Androids presents: Angrybaby
494.description: On Saturday, April 11th at Culture, Angrybaby brings his high-velocity, bass-driven sound straight from the Bay Area, blending punchy house with UK-leaning club grooves, razor-sharp drums, and instant payoff drops.  He first broke through in 2023 with his debut EP REGULARITY via TH3RD BRAIN Records, and has kept the momentum with recent releases like HYPERMOTION plus heavy hitters including I NEED YOU and PUT IT DOWN built to snap a dancefloor into motion fast. With massive performances at Splash House 2025 and Escape Halloween 2025, expect a locked in, high impact set with zero downtime.  Do not miss out on this one.

560.title: Reasons to Gather: Japanese Tea Practice Unwrapped
560.description: This exhibition presents eleven historic tea utensils and accessories that tell a story of exchange from across Asia to Japanese tea rooms.
560.venue_name: Smithsonian National Museum of Asian Art

561.title: Shifting Boundaries: Perspectives on American Landscapes
561.description: This exhibition looks at American landscapes from a new angle by engaging a multiplicity of voices and viewpoints to explore what these paintings can tell us about changes to this region both past and present.
561.venue_name: Smithsonian National Museum of Asian Art

562.title: Knotted Clay: Raku Ceramics and Tea
562.description: This exhibition features tea bowls, water containers, and other vessels in the museum’s permanent collection that demonstrate the glazes and forms unique to Raku ware, and explores these distinctive, hand-molded ceramics and their close relationship to Japanese tea culture.
562.venue_name: Smithsonian National Museum of Asian Art

563.title: Ruffled Feathers: Creating Whistler’s Peacock Room
563.description: From the moment of its creation, the Peacock Room has been a personal, artistic, and cultural battleground.
563.venue_name: Smithsonian National Museum of Asian Art

564.title: The Art of Knowing in South Asia, Southeast Asia, and the Himalayas
564.description: This exhibition brings together highlights from our collections to explore religious and practical knowledge across time, space, and cultures.
564.venue_name: Smithsonian National Museum of Asian Art

565.title: Connecting Stories at the National Museum of Asian Art
565.description: American Art, Ancient Egyptian Art, Ancient Near Eastern Art, Arts of the Islamic World, Chinese Art, Japanese Art, Korean Art, South Asian & Himalayan Art
565.venue_name: Smithsonian National Museum of Asian Art

566.title: Do Ho Suh: Public Figures
566.description: Artist Do Ho Suh was commissioned to create a special edition of his work Public Figures to be installed in front of the museum and facing the National Mall in Washington, DC.
566.venue_name: Smithsonian National Museum of Asian Art

567.title: The Peacock Room in Blue and White
567.description: Whistler envisioned variations of the peacock and its plumage in blue, green, and gold as the ideal backdrop for Chinese porcelains.
567.venue_name: Smithsonian National Museum of Asian Art

591.title: Basquiat × Banksy - Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
591.description: Basquiat×Banksyis an exhibition of two major paintings, one by Jean-Michel Basquiat (b. Brooklyn, New York, 1960–1988) and the other by Banksy (anonymous; b. near Bristol, England). Placed in dialogue, Basquiat’sBoy and Dog in a Johnnypump(1982) and Banksy’s response,Banksquiat. Boy and Dog in Stop and Search(2018), reveal throughlines among street art, contemporary art, and the popular imagination.
591.venue_name: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden

592.title: Revolutions: Art from the Hirshhorn Collection, 1860–1960 - Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
592.description: In celebration of its 50th-anniversary season, the Smithsonian’s Hirshhorn Museum presentsRevolutions: Art from the Hirshhorn Collection, 1860–1960, a major survey of artwork made during a transformative period characterized by new currents in science and philosophy and ever-increasing mechanization.Revolutionscaptures shifting cultural landscapes through the largely chronological presentation of 208 artworks made by 122 artists—including Francis Bacon, Jean Dubuffet, Lee Krasner, Wilfredo Lam, Georgia O’Keeffe, Pablo Picasso, and Jackson Pollock—during these turbulent and energetic years. Filling the Outer Ring galleries on the Museum’s second floor, the works are all part of the Hirshhorn’s permanent collection, and many were gifts to the nation from founding donor Joseph H. Hirshhorn.
592.venue_name: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden

593.title: Mark Bradford: Pickett’s Charge - Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden
593.description: “Politically and socially, we are at the edge of another precipice. I’m standing in the middle of a question about where we are as a nation.”—Mark Bradford
593.venue_name: Smithsonian Hirshhorn Museum and Sculpture Garden

740.title: Running of the Chihuahuas
740.description: Small Dogs, Big Fun. The 14th Annual Running of the Chihuahuas includes a day of fun, festivities and fundraising.

741.title: Nü Androids Presents: X CLUB.
741.description:
X Club. @ Culture 

On Saturday, March 28th, X CLUB. takes over Culture. The Australian duo are known for their high-BPM blend of trance, techno, and hard house, channeling early 2000s rave energy into a modern, warehouse-focused sound. With releases on labels including Steel City Dance Discs and their own imprint, they’ve built a reputation for fast, driving productions and emotionally charged breakdowns that have earned support across Europe and beyond. Their sets move between euphoric trance melodies and pounding techno rhythms with relentless pace, bringing a raw, high-intensity energy to Culture for a night centered on speed, tension, and full dancefloor release.

1025.title: Star Power: Photographs from Hollywood’s Golden Age by George Hurrell (A Sequel)
1025.description: During the 1930s and early 1940s, George Hurrell (1904–1992) reigned as Hollywood’s preeminent portrait photographer.

1026.title: Teen Portrait Competition, 2025
1026.description: The Smithsonian’s National Portrait Gallery has announced the winners of the 2025 Teen Portrait Competition, a triennial event inspired by the museum’s Outwin Boochever Portrait Competition. The selected works showcase the next wave of contemporary portraiture by teens.

1027.title: The Outwin 2025: American Portraiture Today
1027.description: “The Outwin 2025: American Portraiture Today” will present the latest in contemporary portraiture selected from the museum’s triennial Outwin Boochever Portraiture Competition in an exhibition dedicated to the genre’s ability to engage history and illuminate contemporary experience.

1028.title: Portrait of a Nation: 2025 Honorees
1028.description: Established in 2015, the Portrait of a Nation Awards honor extraordinary individuals who have made transformative contributions to the United States and its people.

1029.title: American Winners: Athletes and Entertainers Who Shaped the Nation
1029.description: Athletic performance and creative expression are hallmarks of the American experience. The stories of talented individuals taking to the ﬁeld or gracing center stage have connected us for centuries. When we learn about the grit of those defying gravity, breaking records, or dazzling audiences, we are reminded of our national character and shared humanity. American Winners salutes a diverse array of athletes and entertainers by presenting more than ﬁfty works from the National Portrait Gallery’s permanent collection.

1030.title: From Shadow to Substance: Grand-Scale Portraits During Photography’s Formative Years
1030.description: Photographers seeking customers during the medium’s early years often urged the public to “Secure the shadow, ere the substance fade.” Hinting at life’s fragility, this tagline underscored photography’s ability to capture a fleeting likeness and preserve it for posterity.

1031.title: Meserve Collection Highlights: Modern Prints from Mathew Brady’s Portrait Negatives
1031.description: The Portrait Gallery’s vast collection of Civil War-era, portrait negatives is represented in this exhibition through nine modern prints from Mathew Brady’s (c. 1823–1896) original photographic negatives. Portraits of Abraham Lincoln, Nathaniel Hawthorne, Ulysses Grant, and Emma, Queen of Hawai’i are featured, along with an original, glass-plate negative and one of Brady’s wooden storage boxes.

1032.title: Isaac Julien:  Lessons of the Hour—Frederick Douglass
1032.description: The moving image installation interweaves period reenactments across five screens to create a vivid picture of 19th-century activist, writer, orator and philosopher Frederick Douglass.

1033.title: Abraham Lincoln by W.F.K. Travers
1033.description: The National Portrait Gallery is proud to announce the installation of a life-size painting of President Abraham Lincoln by artist W.F.K. Travers.

1034.title: 20th Century Americans
1034.description: The Portrait Gallery’s vast collection of Civil War-era, portrait negatives is represented in this exhibition through nine modern prints from Mathew Brady’s (c. 1823–1896) original photographic negatives. Portraits of Abraham Lincoln, Nathaniel Hawthorne, Ulysses Grant, and Emma, Queen of Hawai’i are featured, along with an original, glass-plate negative and one of Brady’s wooden storage boxes.

1173.title: Portrait Gallery Kids
1173.description: Explore the museum's diverse and wide-ranging exhibitions. Thousands of works of art, artifacts and archival materials are available for the study of portraiture. Visitors of all ages can learn about portraiture through a variety of weekly public programs to create art, tell stories, and explore the museum.

1185.title: Finding Awe: Georgia O’Keeffe’sA Black Bird with Snow-Covered Red Hills
1185.description: How do artists help us reflect on the interconnectedness of the world? Learn how awe fuels environmental action while exploring Chakaia Booker’s rubber sculptures.

1186.title: Finding Awe: Chakaia Booker’s Monumental Sculptures

1191.title: Mary Cassatt
1191.description: An intimate exhibition brings together rarely-seen treasures and iconic works by Mary Cassatt, marking 100 years since her death.

1192.title: Celebrating American Art
1192.description: Step into a fresh take on our American art galleries.

1193.title: Back and Forth
1193.description: Spanning six centuries and different cultures, four paintings reveal how artists engage with art history—and become part of it.

1194.title: In the Library: Mary Cassatt’s American Legacy
1194.description: The National Gallery of Art Library presents an installation of archival photographs, rare catalogs, and documentation—including Cassatt’s correspondence—that explore the artist’s American ties.

1195.title: In the Tower: Chakaia Booker
1195.description: See three awe-inspiring sculptures by an American artist who transforms tires to explore environmental concerns.

1196.title: Clifford Ross
1196.description: Our central, soaring Rotunda comes alive with a 23-foot screen featuring waves built from millions of glowing pixels.

1197.title: Dear America
1197.description: What does it mean to be American? How have artists examined this question?

1198.title: Sky Hopinka
1198.description: Through photographs, poems, and film, Sky Hopinka connects family, language, place, and lived Indigenous experience.

1199.title: Niagara Falls
1199.description: Some 20 works probe how the layered histories and meanings of Niagara Falls have evolved from the early 19th century to today.

1200.title: Beneath the Surface
1200.description: Nearly two centuries of American photographs transport us to unexpected places to witness what powers our lives.

1201.title: American Icon
1201.description: 30 works from the late 1800s to today illustrate changing uses and interpretations of the US flag, the values it represents, and the people who identify with it.

1202.title: Broken
1202.description: Journey to pivotal places and moments in history with sculptures marked by time, war, and reinvention from 2,500 BCE to today. Some 75 works tell stories about resilience, the loss and damage of cultural heritage, and the power and politics of restitution. And many rarely travel; seeing them side by side is a once-in-a-generation opportunity. Brokeninvites you to rethink what “broken” means—to see how fragments have been sources of power, resistance, and artistic inspiration for millennia. You’ll explore how humans and works of art are both powerful and fragile. You may leave seeing the past, present, and yourself a little differently.

1203.title: Alexander Calder
1203.description: Immerse yourself in the world’s largest display of works by Calder, featuring more than 40 sculptures and paintings.

1204.title: American Places
1204.description: See how early 20th-century artists captured experiences of modern life, from isolated mountaintops to crowded cities.

1205.title: Marc Chagall
1205.description: Hidden in a corner of our Sculpture Garden stands a large-scale mosaic which combines narratives from Greek mythology with the artist's personal experience escaping Nazi-occupied France.

1206.title: Amedeo Modigliani
1206.description: A rare opportunity to see 11 paintings and one sculpture by the artist together.

1207.title: Mark Rothko
1207.description: Experience the rare chance to see paintings from across Rothko’s career, from early paintings of bodies to his monumental abstract canvases.

1208.title: Barnett Newman
1208.description: This gallery presents what many consider American painter Barnett Newman’s greatest achievement.

1209.title: Room-Like
1209.description: Immerse yourself in works that reimagine room-like spaces.

1210.title: The Corcoran Collection
1210.description: This room honors the Corcoran Gallery of Art, the first institution in the United States created specifically as an art museum.

1211.title: The Robert and Jane Meyerhoff Collection
1211.description: See a selection of post-World War II American art ranging from expressionist to pop and abstract to figurative.

1212.title: The Shaw 54th Regiment Memorial
1212.description: See a plaster version of Augustus Saint-Gaudens’s revolutionary memorial to the 54th Massachusetts Regiment, one of the first African American regiments formed in the North.

1213.title: Thomas Cole
1213.description: See the Hudson River School painter's four-part series tracing a pilgrim’s journey through changing landscapes along the “River of Life” from childhood to old age.

1214.title: Masterpieces of American Furniture from the Kaufman Collection, 1700–1830
1214.description: An installation of nearly 100 examples of early American furniture and decorative arts.

1215.title: Women and the Index of American Design
1215.description: Nearly 50 drawings examine women’s roles in the Depression-era art project that sought to document American designs in applied, decorative, and folk art.

1221.title: (Re)Discover the National Gallery of Art
1221.description: Whether this is your first time discovering the National Gallery or we are welcoming you back, this free tour is for you!

1222.title: Italian Renaissance Collection
1222.description: How did artists respond to the culture and ideas of the Renaissance in Italy?Join us in exploring the rise of humanism in society and innovations in art through works by artists like Giotto, Leonardo da Vinci, Raphael, and Titian.

1223.title: American Stories
1223.description: Join us as we look closely at American art from the colonial period to the early 20th century, engaging in conversation and exploring different perspectives with these works from our collection.

1224.title: Dialogues in Modern and Contemporary Art
1224.description: Discover connections and “dialogues” between modern and contemporary works of art in the National Gallery’s East Building. Together we will explore artistic process and uncover layers of meaning in painting and sculpture through close looking and conversation.

1225.title: French Art: Impressionism to the Early 20th Century
1225.description: On this tour of the National Gallery of Art’s collection, discover the ways 19th-century French artists broke with tradition and transformed French art. Together we will look closely and explore how artists such as Claude Monet, Paul Cézanne, and Mary Cassatt challenged the art establishment through their subject matter and techniques.

1226.title: French Art: Impressionism to the Early 20th Century
1226.description: On this tour of the National Gallery of Art’s collection, discover the ways 19th-century French artists broke with tradition and transformed French art. Together we will look closely and explore how artists such as Claude Monet, Paul Cézanne, and Mary Cassatt challenged the art establishment through their subject matter and techniques.

1227.title: (Re)Discover the National Gallery of Art
1227.description: Whether this is your first time discovering the National Gallery or we are welcoming you back, this free tour is for you!

1228.title: Italian Renaissance Collection
1228.description: How did artists respond to the culture and ideas of the Renaissance in Italy?Join us in exploring the rise of humanism in society and innovations in art through works by artists like Giotto, Leonardo da Vinci, Raphael, and Titian.

1229.title: Glimpses of Life in 17th Century Dutch Art
1229.description: Join us to explore works of art that offer glimpses of life in 17th century Dutch society during a period of social, economic, and religious change.

1230.title: Exhibition Tour—Back and Forth: Rozeal., Titian, Cezanne
1230.description: Join us for an interactive conversation about Back and Forth: Rozeal., Titian, Cezanne.

1231.title: Breaking the Rules in Modern Art
1231.description: How and why did rules of painting and sculpture change radically throughout the 20th century? Through close looking and conversation, we'll explore how artists departed from traditional materials and subject matter while grappling with the modern world.

1232.title: French Art: Impressionism to the Early 20th Century
1232.description: On this tour of the National Gallery of Art’s collection, discover the ways 19th-century French artists broke with tradition and transformed French art. Together we will look closely and explore how artists such as Claude Monet, Paul Cézanne, and Mary Cassatt challenged the art establishment through their subject matter and techniques.

1239.description: Whether this is your first time discovering the National Gallery or we are welcoming you back, this free tour is for you!

1240.description: How did artists respond to the culture and ideas of the Renaissance in Italy?Join us in exploring the rise of humanism in society and innovations in art through works by artists like Giotto, Leonardo da Vinci, Raphael, and Titian.

1241.title: Glimpses of Life in 17th Century Dutch Art
1241.description: Join us to explore works of art that offer glimpses of life in 17th century Dutch society during a period of social, economic, and religious change.

1242.title: French Sculpture
1242.description: Explore 19th century French sculptures from the National Gallery’s collection, including groundbreaking bronzes and marble sculptures by Rodin and rare wax figures by Degas such as Little Dancer Aged Fourteen. Together we will examine materials, techniques, subject matter, and artistic intent.

1243.title: Breaking the Rules in Modern Art
1243.description: How and why did rules of painting and sculpture change radically throughout the 20th century? Through close looking and conversation, we'll explore how artists departed from traditional materials and subject matter while grappling with the modern world.

1255.title: (Re)Discover the National Gallery of Art
1255.description: Whether this is your first time discovering the National Gallery or we are welcoming you back, this free tour is for you!

1257.title: Hair, Paper, Water
1257.description: Join us for a special screening of Hair, Paper, Water (Tóc, Giấy và Nước…) in partnership with this year’s DC Environmental Film Festival.

1258.title: The Treasure of the Sierra Madre
1258.description: This searing tale of greed, paranoia, and brotherhood follows actors Humphrey Bogart and Walter Huston on their desperate search for gold in Mexico. Gritty, darkly humorous, and psychologically precise, this Academy Award-winning film is one of Hollywood’s most unsparing moral dramas. The Treasure of the Sierra Madre was selected for the National Film Registry in 1990. (John Huston, 1948, 35mm, 126 minutes)

1259.title: Santa Fe Trail
1259.description: Entertaining yet historically inaccurate, the Oscar-winning fable of Santa Fe Trail features iconic Hollywood actors Ronald Reagan, Erroll Flynn, Raymond Massey, and Olivia de Havilland in a sweeping retelling of the buildup to the US Civil War. (Michael Curtiz, 1940, 35mm, 110 minutes)

1260.title: The Three Stooges: Rockin’ in the Rockies followed by Rin Tin Tin in Clash of the Wolves
1260.description: Rockin’ in the Rockies is a rollicking Western musical featuring the Three Stooges. Left to care for a ranch owned by his cousin, “Shorty” enlists vagrants Curly and Larry to join him as bumbling prospectors intent on mining the land. Joined by the Hoosier Hotshots quartet, the Stooges and their compadres eventually make it big. (Vernon Keayes, 1945, 35mm, 63 minutes) Followed by Clash of the Wolves : “Filmed in the deserts of California (in what is now the Joshua Tree National Park), Clash of the Wolves marks the cinematic pinnacle of the original Rin Tin Tin (Rinty), a German Shepherd rescued from a World War I battlefield and brought to America where he became a screen sensation. Rinty stars as Lobo, leader of a pack of wolves hunted by the authorities. Through an act of kindness, prospector Dave Weston earns Lobo’s trust. When a claim-jumper attempts to murder Weston, Lobo performs extraordinary feats to demonstrate the depths of his loyalty” – Susan Orlean Selected for the National Film Registry in 2004, Clash of the Wolves is restored in 4K and is presented with the recording of a new musical score composed and performed by Ben Model. (Noel Smith, 1925, 35mm, 68 minutes)

1261.title: National Gallery Nights: United We Create
1261.description: Join us for an immersive evening where the visual soul of America meets the rhythms of American music.

1262.title: Chinatown Street Photography Workshop
1262.description: This is a 4 hour street photography workshop in Chinatown and it is limited to 5 people. We spend the entire time on the street shooting, discussing and reviewing. No matter what your level of experience is, amateur to professional, there's something for everyone.

1263.title: Exploring Styles & Techniques in Street - Soho Workshop
1263.description: This is a 4 hour street photography workshop in Soho and it is limited to 5 people. We spend the entire time on the street shooting, discussing and reviewing. No matter what your level of experience is, amateur to professional, there's something for everyone.

1264.title: Midtown Manhattan Street Photography Workshop
1264.description: This is a 4 hour street photography workshop in Midtown Manhattan and it is limited to 5 people. We spend the entire time on the street shooting, discussing and reviewing. No matter what your level of experience is, amateur to professional, there's something for everyone.
